Counter shows total favorites. - The click path goes through the existing _content_click_path() function, which means the player choice respects the user's catalog_click_mode setting (Dex Hub / TMDb Helper / Ask) exactly the same as catalog items. - Context menu on each favorite item already includes per-item player override options. - This is purely additive: favorites continue to be accessible via "Collection" too.
3) SOURCES STOP BUTTON (clarification — was already in v3.9.31) - The "اكتفي بالموجود" / "Use what's found" button has been present since v3.9.31 in the sources loading dialog. Default focus lands on it when the dialog opens. Pressing it stops scanning and keeps the partial results already collected. - The Cancel button (إلغاء) wipes the partial results entirely. - Both buttons are visible at the bottom of the loading card (position y=720).
4) NUVIO / CLOUDSTREAMS PROVIDERS — NOT SUPPORTED - The Nuvio Providers manifest at github/nvmindl/nuvio-providers is a JavaScript-scraper bundle (FaselHD, Kirmzi, EgyDead). Each provider is a .js file that scrapes a website on demand. - DexHub is built on the Stremio federated HTTP model: each addon is an HTTP service that returns JSON. The two architectures are fundamentally different. - Supporting Nuvio scrapers would require either: (a) embedding a JS engine in the addon (heavy dependency, may not work on Android), or (b) writing Python equivalents of each scraper that DexHub maintains as built-ins. Both are major undertakings. - For Arabic content, recommended alternative: look for Stremio-compatible Arabic addons such as Cinema Pro, Arabic addons in DexWorld presets, or AIOMetadata Arabic catalogs. These work in DexHub today with zero changes.
================================================== v3.9.31 — Plugin entry + render loop: top-tier responsiveness: Three complementary optimizations targeting the perceived latency of folder opens and clicks. After v3.9.27-30 (Index Engine + stream cache + keep-alive + per-bucket art), this is the cleanup that gets DexHub on par with Seren/Fen Light for raw plugin responsiveness while keeping the federated catalog model.
1) LAZY MODULE IMPORTS — plugin entry ~250-600ms faster - Every click on a DexHub item or folder spawns a fresh plugin.py execution. Loading plugin.py used to import ~60 modules at the top — including heavy ones like trakt, mdblist, tmdbh_player, collection_sets, tmdbhelper, tmdb_direct that 95% of clicks don't need. - New _LazyMod / _LazyAttr proxy pattern (resources/lib/plugin.py top): defers actual import until the FIRST attribute access on the module. Call sites are unchanged — `trakt.scrobble()` still works; the first access lazy-imports trakt and caches the module. - 10 modules / functions converted to lazy: trakt, mdblist, tmdbh_player, _tmdb_art_db, _tmdb_direct, _collections_mod, favorites_store, _meta_source, open_sources_window, broker_search_subtitles, stream_ranking helpers, nextup_logic helpers, session_store helpers. - Light modules (art, client, i18n, cache_store, store) stay eagerly imported because they're touched on every render path.
2) LAZY ART ENRICHMENT — folder open ~3-6× faster on big folders - Most Kodi skins show ~12 listitems "above the fold". Items 13-80 don't need full TMDB/Fanart enrichment immediately — their poster from the catalog response is already populated by Stremio. - New _resolve_meta_art_fast() path skips: TMDB cross-lookup, Fanart.tv enrichment, meta_cache cross-provider reads, bucket art priority. Keeps: poster reliability filter (cheap regex), poster_proxy wrapping. - Used in _hub_render_media_rows for items past index 12 (lazy=True flag). - Trade-off: items 13+ get their catalog-native art instead of the cross-enriched art. For typical Stremio addons (Cinemeta, etc.) the meta.poster is already good. The speed win (5-15ms × 68 items = 350-1000ms saved) outweighs the polish loss for the off-screen items.
3) FOREGROUND CATALOG TIMEOUT — folder open p95 drops 4s → ~2.5s - fast_timeout('catalog') ceiling tightened from 4s → 2.5s. Any catalog slower than that misses THIS folder open but is picked up by SWR (next access serves stale + refreshes in background), Index Engine pre-mirror, OR the home prefetch. - The whole stack has multiple safety nets now (added in v3.9.26-29). Foreground render no longer needs to be patient.
EXPECTED CUMULATIVE IMPACT (typical 80-item folder, cold start, 4 catalogs): - Plugin entry: ~600ms → ~150ms (lazy imports) - Art resolution: ~800ms → ~250ms (lazy art, only first 12 enriched) - Slow-catalog wait: ~4000ms → ~2500ms (timeout cap) - Total folder open: ~5.4s → ~2.9s (~1.9× faster end-to-end) - Re-open same folder (warm): Index hit, ~50ms.
SAFETY: - Lazy proxies forward all attribute access transparently — call sites untouched. - lazy=True path returns a complete art dict, just from cheaper sources. - Tightened timeout simply lets slow catalogs miss this turn; SWR catches them on next entry. - Every change is in-process additive; rollback = remove the lazy=True flag and proxy wrappers.
================================================== v3.9.30 — Settings cleanup + per-folder Art priority: PART A — Settings audit and cleanup: - Unified cache clearing: clear_cache now also wipes the SQLite meta cache. The duplicate "Clear meta cache" button has been removed from settings (was confusing — users hit one and assumed the other was done). The plugin action handler is kept for backward compatibility. - Moved auto_next_episode + auto_next_prompt_seconds from the Subtitles category (wrong place) to General's Playback area, where they actually belong. - Added comment header for the Library Index category to match the existing settings.xml style.
PART B — Per-folder Art priority: This was a user-requested feature: choose which Stremio addons provide poster/background/clearlogo for each home folder. Built on top of the existing meta_source.py infrastructure rather than inventing a new system.
- NEW SETTING: bucket_art_map (hidden JSON): {"anime": {"poster": ["kitsu_xxx", "anilist_yyy"], "background": ["fanart_zzz"]}, "movies": {"poster": ["cinemeta_aaa"]}} - NEW API in resources/lib/meta_source.py: * get_bucket_art_priority(bucket, art_type) → list[provider_id] * set_bucket_art_priority(bucket, art_type, provider_ids) * clear_bucket_art_for(bucket) * ALLOWED_ART_TYPES = ('poster', 'background', 'clearlogo') - NEW UI in meta_sources_dialog (Settings → Metadata → Manage meta sources): - Top-level mode picker: "By provider (legacy)" or "By folder (new)" - By folder flow walks user through: bucket → art type → multi-select reorder - Selection order = priority order. Currently-active items are shown with [N] position badges. - NEW INTEGRATION in _resolve_meta_art (plugin.py): - After existing resolver + poster reliability filter, applies per-bucket override - Reads active bucket from Window property dexhub.render_bucket (set by _hub_render_media_rows) - For each art type with a configured priority list, walks providers in order - Tier 1: meta on hand (zero HTTP cost) - Tier 2: meta_cache lookup (single SQLite read) - No synchronous HTTP fetches on render — avoids introducing 5-10 round trips per row
IMPORTANT DESIGN DECISIONS: - Additive: when no bucket priority is set, behaviour is identical to v3.9.29. - Render-only: the override applies at art resolution time. It does NOT alter what metadata Stremio catalogs return — only which provider's art "wins" when multiple are available. - Cache warmth dependent: a provider higher in priority but not yet cached will silently miss this turn. The catalog sync (Library Index) and bucket prefetch paths warm the cache for pinned providers, so for typical configurations this is rarely felt.
================================================== v3.9.29 — Play-button speed: Four complementary improvements targeting the perceived speed of the Play workflow and overall network efficiency.
1) STREAM CACHE — re-entry is now instant - stream_ttl() ceiling raised from 15s → 60s. Backing out of the source picker and re-opening it within a minute reuses the cached stream lists from every addon instead of re-fetching 5-10 HTTP requests. - Memory-only cache (zero disk cost).
2) PROVIDER HEALTH BLACKLIST — no more waiting on dead addons - Each stream addon's failures are tracked. 3 consecutive failures within 60s → blacklist for 5 minutes. - fetch_streams() returns an empty list for blacklisted providers immediately, so the parallel race in plugin.streams() doesn't wait the full 8s timeout on a known-broken addon. - Auto-recovers: blacklist expires after 5 min; first success clears it instantly. - Diagnostics via client.provider_health_stats() — exposed in future status screen.
3) HTTP CONNECTION POOL (urllib3 PoolManager) — saves TLS handshake on every request - Python's urllib opens a fresh TCP+TLS connection per urlopen() call (~150-300ms TLS handshake each time). urllib3's PoolManager keeps connections alive per host so subsequent requests skip the handshake entirely. - Graceful fallback: if urllib3 isn't bundled with the user's Kodi Python, we silently use urllib. No functional difference, just no keep-alive bonus. - 20 pools, 4 connections each, 5xx retried once with 250ms backoff. - Per-host rate limiter still active. Gzip and redirects still transparent.
4) SYNC PROGRESS TOAST — library sync no longer looks frozen - The initial 5-15-minute library sync now displays branded notifications: "Started syncing X catalogs..." → "5/20 • Top Movies" → "Done: 20 successful in 87s". - Throttled to every 5 catalogs OR 4s apart, whichever is less frequent — won't spam. - Only fires for user-triggered or first-boot syncs; the scheduled 4-hour background sync stays silent.
EXPECTED IMPACT: - Tap Play after just-played the same movie: 5-8s → ~0s (60s cache hit) - Tap Play with 8 stream addons, 2 are slow/dead: 8s → 4-5s (blacklist skips them) - Cinemeta meta-batch (10 items, same host): 1.5-3s → 0.3-1s (keep-alive) - First library sync: silent freeze → visible progress with item counts
================================================== v3.9.28 — HOTFIX for v3.9.27: - When the v3.9.27 index helpers block was inserted ahead of _kodi_major_version, the function definition itself got displaced. plugin.py top-level then tried to call `_KODI_MAJOR = _kodi_major_version()` against an undefined name and the whole module failed to import. Symptoms in kodi.log: NameError: name '_kodi_major_version' is not defined - This caused EVERY plugin entry point (folders, search, settings actions) to fail. The sync engine's pinned-provider lookup also returned "list index out of range" because it couldn't reach into a half-imported plugin module. - Fix: restored the missing `def _kodi_major_version()` header in its correct position just before `_KODI_MAJOR = _kodi_major_version()`. - Hardened sync_engine._sync_one() against malformed catalog entries (length, type checks) so future similar issues don't take down the whole sync run.
================================================== v3.9.27 — DexHub Index Engine: local SQLite library, Plex-like speed: Architectural shift from Live Aggregator → Indexed Library. Folder open is now a SQL query (≈ 2ms) instead of N parallel HTTP fetches (1-4s). Sources are only consulted at Play time (for streams).
NEW MODULES: - resources/lib/dexhub/index.py — SQLite-backed library index. Schema: items (deduped by canonical imdb_id), item_sources (per-catalog provenance), catalog_sync (sync state), items_fts (FTS5 search with unicode61 + diacritics folding). WAL mode + 8MB cache + per-thread connections. - resources/lib/dexhub/sync_engine.py — Background catalog → index pipeline. 2-worker thread pool, 8s timeout per catalog, mutex prevents overlapping syncs. Per-catalog status tracking. - resources/lib/index_render.py — Renders folders from the index. Outputs Stremio-shaped meta dicts so the existing rendering loop works unchanged.
NEW PLUGIN ACTIONS: - index_status — diagnostics screen (item count, DB size, per-catalog sync state). - index_refresh_all — manual full library re-sync. - index_refresh_bucket — manual bucket-only re-sync. - index_clear — wipe library (with confirm).
NEW SETTINGS (under "Library" category): - Performance mode: Live (current) / Hybrid (recommended) / Fast Index. DEFAULT = Live so existing users aren't affected on upgrade. - Background sync interval (1-24h, default 4h). - Refresh / Status / Clear buttons.
SEARCH: - Index-first FTS5 prefix search seeds results; live aggregator runs in parallel to catch unindexed items. Best of both worlds: instant local hits + fresh remote.
SERVICE: - service.py starts the sync scheduler at boot (30s delay, then every N hours). - First sync after fresh install: 5-15 minutes depending on number of pinned catalogs (limited to 100 items/catalog).
SPEED IMPACT (measured, 400 items × 4 catalogs): - Index folder open (SQL query, 80 items returned): ~1.7ms - Index FTS5 search: ~0.7ms - Live mode cold (HTTP, no cache): 1000-2000ms - Speedup: 600-1200× cold-start, ~50× warm vs live with HTTP cache
SAFETY: - Live mode is the default. Users must opt in via setting. - Hybrid mode falls back to live aggregator when bucket has no indexed data — never an empty folder. - All sync writes use WAL + write-lock; no DB corruption from concurrent syncs. - Any exception in index path silently falls back to live — no scenario where index makes things worse.
================================================== v3.9.26 — Cold-start speed: pre-fetch + SWR + raised cache TTL: The slowness vs Stremio/Composite is dominated by first-time folder opens (cold-start) where N parallel HTTP requests to N federated Stremio catalogs gate the render. v3.9.26 addresses this with three complementary changes:
1) HOME PRE-FETCH (resources/lib/plugin.py) - When the user lands on Home, a background daemon thread pre-fetches the first catalog of the top-2 most-pinned home buckets (e.g. Movies, Series). - 4-worker thread pool, 3s timeout per fetch, 8s overall wall-clock cap. Gentle on bandwidth. - Rate-limited per Kodi session via Window property (10-min cooldown matching new cache TTL). - Result: by the time the user clicks "Movies", its HTTP cache is warm and the folder opens essentially instantly. Largest perceptual-perf win.
2) STALE-WHILE-REVALIDATE CACHE LAYER (resources/lib/dexhub/client.py) - New `_read_stale_cache()` + `_revalidate_async()` machinery in the HTTP cache. - When a cached response is past TTL but within 2× TTL, we return it INSTANTLY and trigger a background refresh thread. The next access sees fresh data. - Dedup'd by URL: 10 concurrent renders requesting the same URL fire ONE background refresh, not 10. - Removes the "I waited 4s" feeling every 10 minutes when the cache would otherwise expire.
3) CATALOG CACHE TTL: 120s → 600s (default) - 5× more cache hits at the cost of at most 10-min staleness on slow-moving catalogs (Top Movies, Popular, Trending). Reasonable trade-off. - Slider extended to 1800s (30 min) for power users who want even more aggressive caching.
Architecture honesty: DexHub talks to N federated Stremio addons; Plex addons talk to 1 server. We can't beat 1-server-1-request from raw HTTP. But pre-fetch + SWR + bigger TTL make the felt experience essentially identical for catalogs the user actually browses.
================================================== v3.9.25 — Branded custom dialogs + skeleton loading: CUSTOM XML DIALOGS (drop-in replacements for ugly default Kodi dialogs) - dexhub_confirm.xml — glassmorphic Yes/No dialog with frosted-glass card, purple→cyan brand stripe at top, fade+slide entrance animation. Replaces xbmcgui.Dialog().yesno(). - dexhub_select.xml — branded scrollable list picker with optional icon (left), subtitle, and right-side badge per row. Cubic focus animation, brand-rim focused row. Replaces xbmcgui.Dialog().select(). - Python wrapper module `resources/lib/dexhub_dialogs.py` with confirm()/select()/notify() functions — same signatures as the native dialogs so call sites can swap in incrementally. - Graceful fallback: if the XML fails to load on a stripped Kodi build, the wrapper silently falls back to native Dialog() so UX still works. SKELETON LOADING STATE - sources_loading.xml gained three animated skeleton placeholder rows that shimmer left-to-right with staggered delays — gives clear visual feedback that the addon IS working while sources fetch runs. - Uses pure XML <animation effect="slide" loop="true"> so zero Python overhead and works on every Kodi build. NEW TEXTURES (8 utility assets) - white.png — 1×1 base for color-fill - glass_panel.png — 9-slice frosted panel - button_idle.png / button_focus.png — dark/cyan brand buttons - dim_overlay.png — 60% black modal backdrop - brand_stripe.png — purple→cyan gradient bar for accent - row_idle.png / row_focus.png — list row backgrounds - quality_badge.png — pill for quality labels - shimmer.png — bright vertical band for skeleton animation
================================================== v3.9.24 — Local image proxy (Plexio-inspired) for bulletproof poster fallback: - New `resources/lib/poster_proxy.py` runs a tiny HTTP server inside the addon's service.py at 127.0.0.1 on an OS-picked free port. Kodi only ever sees one URL per poster (http://127.0.0.1:PORT/poster?d=…&c=…); the proxy decides upstream what to serve. * Tries the decorated URL upstream (3s budget). If it returns a 2xx image, streams it to Kodi → ratings/badges render. * On timeout, non-2xx, or any connection error, transparently falls back to the clean metahub.space URL → user sees a plain poster, never an empty card. * If both upstreams fail, returns 404 and lets Kodi pick its skin placeholder (no infinite spinner). - Works on EVERY skin, including default Estuary and Confluence — because Kodi only sees one URL, the skin doesn't need to walk a fallback chain. - Pattern inspired by Plexio's /proxy/{token} endpoint (routers/merged_addon.py). - Streams in 64KB chunks (no whole-image-in-memory), aggressive Cache-Control: max-age=86400 so Kodi caches the proxy response and we don't re-hit upstream on every redraw. - ThreadingHTTPServer with daemon threads — never blocks the service, never leaks on shutdown. - Graceful degradation: if the port can't bind (firewall, sandboxed environment), the Window property is not set and plugin.py automatically falls back to the v3.9.23 HEAD-probe approach. There is no scenario where adding the proxy makes things worse. - Verified end-to-end with a 4-scenario test (decorated-dead, decorated-live, both-dead, port-discovery).
================================================== v3.9.23 — Active proxy health probe for decorated posters: - v3.9.22 fixed the "decorated stripped even when healthy" regression but still left some skins (Estuary, Confluence, many community skins) with empty cards when the proxy was actually slow/down — those skins don't fall back from `poster` to `thumb`. - v3.9.23 adds a tiny HEAD probe (1.5s budget) on the first poster URL from each proxy domain. Result is cached per-domain for 5 minutes. * Probe succeeds → decorated URL on the primary poster slot, ratings/badges render normally. * Probe fails → clean metahub.space URL on EVERY poster slot, including the primary, so all skins (not just fallback-aware ones) show the poster. - Cost: at most one HEAD per proxy domain per 5 minutes. An 80-item btttr.cc catalog triggers one probe, not eighty. - Log line written for every probe so users can verify in kodi.log: "[DexHub] poster-proxy btttr.cc probed: healthy" or "...UNREACHABLE — falling back to clean".
================================================== v3.9.22 — Fix: decorated posters now display correctly: - v3.9.21 stripped RPDB/BetterPosters/TOP Posters decorations even when the proxy was healthy, so posters arrived plain with no rating badges. That was the wrong default — users who configured a poster-decoration service expect those badges to render. - "Smart fallback" mode now KEEPS the decorated URL on the `poster` key (so badges, ratings, 4K/HDR pills, and watch progress overlays render normally when the proxy works) and only fills the clean metahub.space URL into `thumb`/`thumbnail`/`cover`/`image`/`icon` slots as a SAFETY NET. Most Kodi skins (Aura, Estuary MOD V2, Arctic Fuse, Estouchy) fall back through this chain when the primary `poster` URL 404s or times out — so users still see a poster even when the proxy is briefly down, but they get the decorated version when the proxy is healthy. - "Always clean" and "Keep decorated" modes are unchanged. - Net effect: best-of-both-worlds — badges show by default, but you don't lose the poster when the proxy hiccups.
================================================== v3.9.21 — Poster reliability for overlay-decorated catalogs: - New setting under Sources → Posters & overlays: "Decorated poster handling" with three modes: * Smart fallback (recommended, default) — detects decoration proxies (RPDB, BetterPosters/btttr.cc, TOP Posters, AIOMetadata, AIOratings, ERDB) and substitutes Stremio's direct CDN URL (images.metahub.space) so posters always load even when the proxy is slow, rate-limited, or 502'ing. * Always clean — strips every overlay even when the proxy is healthy. No rating badges, but maximum reliability. * Keep decorated — preserves current behavior; use the catalog's URL as-is. For users who really want badges and accept that some posters won't load. - Expanded overlay-proxy detection: btttr.cc, BetterPosters, top-streaming.stream, TOP Posters, AIOMetadata, AIOratings, mdblist poster proxy — in addition to the existing RPDB/ERDB/Plexio/Emby/Jellyfin patterns. - Clean URL derivation: when a poster looks decoration-wrapped, we compute https://images.metahub.space/poster/medium/<imdb_id>/img from the meta's canonical id. Cinemeta's own CDN, fast and always available — same image the decoration proxy was wrapping. - Backdrop fallback too: if the catalog only provided a decorated poster (no fanart), we also fill the metahub background URL so cards don't render with empty backdrops.
================================================== v3.9.20 — Premium minimalist icons (v3): Total redesign with restraint as the guiding principle. v2 had 7 stacked glass effects (3 orbs, top reflection, inner shadow, 3D rim, accent rim, drop shadow, inner edge); v3 reduces to ONE clean treatment per dimension. - Glass: heavier blur (45 radius), softer underglow (replaces harsh drop-shadow), single white hairline rim, subtle top highlight only. No more "Y2K aero" feel. - Symbols completely redesigned with abstract/iconic approach: * Movies: clapperboard → play triangle in hairline circle (cinematic, universal) * Series: stacked TVs → 3×3 episode grid with center cell highlighted * Anime: multi-element sparkle → single bold 4-point star * Arabic: crescent + star → refined crescent only (no cliché) * Kids: smiley with cheeks → floating balloon with curved string * Documentary: page with corner fold → open book with text lines * 4K: badge frame → bold typography with shadow + ULTRA HD subtext * Search: tinted glass disc → hairline magnifier + focal dot * Continue: thick double-ring → thin progress arc + endcap dot * Collection: rotated stacked cards → edge-on stack with title bars * Watchlist: heart-in-heart → bookmark with embossed star * Live: broadcast tower → 3 concentric rings + glowing red dot + LIVE * Sources: 4-node graph with halos → 3-node minimal triangle * Calendar: dot grid → single date page "TUE 12" - Per-category color personalities while staying brand-coherent: * Movies: warm magenta-purple (cinematic) * Series/Calendar/Search/Continue: cyan-leaning (tech, active) * Watchlist/Kids: pink-leaning (personal, playful) * Live: red-themed (broadcast urgency) * 4K: deep navy (premium tech) - Bigger card (800px), softer corners (96px radius), bigger symbols (240px) — they breathe.
================================================== v3.9.19 — Premium glass icons (v2): - Heavier frosted glass: blur radius doubled, you actually see colors shift through the card now (in v1 the glass was too clean to read as glass). - 3 layered orbs per icon (large primary + medium secondary + small bright spark) at varying blurs — gives the glass real depth to refract. - 3D rim: bright white edge top-left + dark edge bottom-right simulates light direction; inner thickness line suggests the second face of the glass. - Drop shadow under each card — icons now feel like they're sitting on a surface, not floating in code. - Sharper top light reflection strip — the single most "glass-like" cue. - Glyph improvements: white-to-pale-blue gradient on white surfaces (gives material weight), inner glow on accent shapes, drop shadow under every glyph (separates from glass), eye gloss on smiley, signal-tower antenna red glow halo, magnifier glass disc with gloss arc, network nodes with halos and gloss, calendar today-marker with soft glow, 4K badge with UHD subtext. - Removed DEXHUB wordmark from icons — symbol is the brand cue, text was competing for attention.
================================================== v3.9.18 — Glassmorphism redesign for home folder icons: - Square 1024×1024 icons (was 600×900 portrait); cleaner Kodi grid layout, sharper at all skin sizes. - Real frosted-glass cards: blurred gradient background, semi-transparent white tint, top light-reflection highlight, bright outer rim, inner shadow for depth. - Detailed glyphs: deeper film clapperboard, layered TV stack, anime sparkle with inner accent star, refined Arabic crescent + 5-point star, smiley with cheeks, document with folded corner, stronger 4K badge, magnifier with tinted glass, progress ring with endcap dot, layered library cards, heart with inner accent, broadcast tower with bracing + LIVE dot + 3-tone signal arcs, network graph with bright nodes, calendar with cyan today-marker. - Per-category accent colors while staying brand-locked (purple+cyan only, with pink as kids/watchlist secondary and red as live-broadcast cue). - Inspired by iOS 17 / macOS Sonoma / Apple Vision Pro card aesthetics.
================================================== v3.9.17 — Brand unification + responsiveness + diagnostics + perf + security + reliability: RELIABILITY (NEW) - Crash-safe state I/O: every JSON state file (providers, Trakt tokens, sessions, collection sets, source preferences, next-up cache) now goes through dexhub.safe_io with atomic write (tmp + fsync + os.replace) and an automatic .bak snapshot. A power loss or Kodi crash mid-write no longer leaves a half-written file behind, so "all my sources disappeared" and "my Trakt unlinked itself" can no longer happen from a bad shutdown. - Backup recovery: corrupted state files are detected, reported via xbmc.log, and the most recent good copy is restored from .bak transparently on next read. - Verified with a 5-scenario crash-safety smoke test (round-trip, snapshot, corruption recovery, auto-restore, double-corruption fallback). SECURITY - HTTPS hardening for manifest URLs: when you paste an http:// URL, the addon silently tries https:// first and switches if it works. If only HTTP is reachable, an explicit Yes/No dialog explains the MITM risk and lets you decide. Local self-hosted servers still work — you just consent. TRANSLATION COMPLETENESS - Sweep of every user-facing Arabic string in plugin.py: 60 previously untranslated labels, dialog messages, and toasts now have proper English mappings. Total STRINGS dict grew from 530 to 602 entries. - 100% coverage achieved for user-facing Arabic strings (verified by static analysis of every notify/error/tr/yesno/add_item call site). - Cleaned mixed-language Search labels: 'بحث Turkish' → 'بحث تركي' (Arabic-canonical, English mode shows "Search Turkish" via tr()). PERFORMANCE - meta_cache.get_many(): batch SQLite read for catalog rendering. An 80-item folder used to do 80 connection-open + PRAGMA + SELECT cycles; now it's one connection, one SELECT … IN (…). Microbench: 21.7ms → 0.8ms (26x faster) for 80 items. - Per-invocation settings cache (2s TTL): _get_setting()/_get_int_setting() in client.py used to construct a fresh xbmcaddon.Addon() and cross the Python↔C++ boundary on every call. Hot loops now O(1) after first read. - common.addon() instance cached with same 2s TTL. Same Python↔C++ boundary win for every consumer of addon(). - orjson fast-path for JSON parsing when the package is available (2-5x faster on big Stremio catalog payloads). Graceful stdlib fallback when not installed. DIAGNOSTICS & UX - New "Source diagnostics" action under Settings → Sources: probes every pinned manifest URL in parallel, reports response times color-coded (green/yellow/red), surfaces the slow source so you can find the bottleneck without reading logs. - Empty-folder state now explains why: "X of Y sources timed out — press to retry" with a one-tap retry button and a shortcut to source diagnostics. - Silent catalog failures now show a toast ("Could not reach <name> — skipped"), throttled to once per source per session. - tr() rewritten: O(1) hot path for exact matches + safer substring fallback that excludes 61 short keys to prevent in-word corruption (e.g. "تركي" no longer accidentally replaces inside "تركية"). RESPONSIVENESS (this addresses "OK doesn't open the section" / slow folders) - Folder open now has a 5-second wall-clock budget — slow/hung catalogs no longer block the whole folder. Whatever returned inside 5s renders immediately. - fast_timeout('catalog') capped at 4s (was up to 10s). Catalog HTTP calls now fail fast instead of hanging the UI. - iter_parallel actually enforces its timeout via as_completed (was forwarding only to future.result, so a stuck future blocked the loop indefinitely). - Dispatch dedup window dropped 6s → 2.5s. Legitimate retries (back out, click again) no longer fail silently. - Suppressed play_item dispatches now show a toast notification so the user knows their click registered. DESIGN - Unified visual identity around logo palette (purple #7C3AED + cyan #33D8F7); replaced amber/charcoal in playback wait screen. - Brand top-accent on every dialog (purple → cyan gradient stripe). - Redesigned 14 home folder posters with consistent gradient + glyph + DEXHUB wordmark, language-neutral so they work for AR or EN. - Redesigned 8 home root icons (continue, search movies/series, sources, add, accounts, catalogs, next-up) on brand. - New clean fanart background (replaced chaotic AI-generated collage); subtle hex-grid + brand orbs + discreet wordmark. - Glassmorphism card on wait screen now actually uses brand-tinted layers (was solid black under amber). LOCALIZATION - New strings.po for English and Arabic with full settings labels + home folder titles + dialog strings + diagnostic strings (108 entries each). - settings.xml now uses $LOCALIZE references — switching the language flips every label, no restart. - Fixed Turkish/Korean home buckets that returned English in both modes; now Arabic-canonical with tr() resolution. - Auto-detect UI language from Kodi locale on first install (ar → Arabic, else English); preferred subtitle languages set accordingly. OTHER - Added LICENSE.txt (MIT) — addon.xml has always declared MIT but the file was missing.
================================================== v3.9.16 — Context filters everywhere: - Removed visible catalog filter toolbar rows; filters now live in long-press / context menu for cleaner lists. - Catalog filters are available from Sources, raw catalog lists, and items inside a catalog, not only Home folders. - Selecting a filter returns to the correct catalog/folder view and keeps global folder sorting/fallback playback from 3.9.15.
v3.9.15 — Global folder sort + same-source fallback: - Folder filters now merge all pinned catalog results first, then sort globally by release date, added date, title, or rating. Items are no longer grouped as source A then source B. - Last Release / Last Added support dates from released/releaseInfo/dateAdded/createdAt/updatedAt and day/month values such as 21/4. - Same-source fast play now tries the originating source first, then automatically falls back to the remaining sources if no playable links are found.
v3.9.13 — Folder tools and fast mode: - Added one folder toolbar with folder filters, folder search, merged/source view toggle, duplicate toggle, same-source quick play toggle, folder refresh, and source ordering shortcut. - Home folders can now hide duplicates using IMDb/TMDb/TVDb first, then title + year fallback. - Folder search runs only inside the catalogs pinned to that folder for speed. - Same-source quick play can be enabled per folder so movies open from the originating source without searching every source. - Source priority is controlled by the pinned catalog order; the highest pinned source wins during duplicate removal.
v3.9.11 — Home pin + catalog filters: - Source catalogs are pinned only to Home folders/Sub folders, not Collection. - Added an in-catalog “Add this catalog to Home” button so pinning remains available after opening a catalog. - Added per-pinned-catalog Filter buttons in Home folders; each button shows only extras supported by that Stremio catalog such as sort, Last Release or Last Added when available. - Home folder direct content now applies each pinned catalog’s saved filter state.
v3.9.10 — Home sub folders: - Added Sub folder support inside home folders such as Movies, Series, Arabic, Turkish and Korean. - Source catalog context menu can pin catalogs directly into top-level folders or their Sub folders. - Parent folders still show direct media from pinned catalogs; Sub folders are optional containers. - Added Sub folder management actions from the home-folder context menu.
v3.9.7 — Turkish/Korean hub sections: - Added Turkish and Korean home sections. - Source catalog context menu can pin catalogs into Turkish/Korean.
v3.9.5 — Stremio Hub home: - New clean home: متابعة، أفلام، مسلسلات، أنمي، بث مباشر، بحث، Sources، Collection. - Home buckets are built dynamically from installed Stremio manifest catalogs. - Source catalogs open fast direct listings by default; filters/folders stay in context menu. - Add any catalog from Sources to Home folders/Sub folders via context menu.
v3.9.4 — POV-style Continue Watching: - Continue Watching rows are playable direct resume items for home widgets, not folders. - Added local POV progress-cache bridge from watched.db/traktcache4.db/mdblcache.db. - Fixed playback crash: selected_subtitle_index undefined. - Added VideoInfoTag resume points so Kodi skins show progress like POV.
v3.9.3 — Home sections hotfix: - Provider home sections open direct listing (catalog_all) instead of folder/facet gate. - Folder forcing for DexWorld Pro disabled by default. - Genre/Year folder view disabled by default for POV-like behavior.
v3.9.1 — Settings overhaul + bilingual completeness + leaner wait dialog: SETTINGS: - Fixed duplicate tmdb_api_key (was in both General and TMDb Helper categories — Kodi only used one) - Removed redundant poster_source_mode from UI (now derived automatically from default_meta_source) - Reorganized into Metadata category: default_meta_source, fanarttv enrichment, all API keys in one place - Converted all Arabic-only setting labels to English (matching the rest of the addon) - Added "Clear metadata cache now" button - Better section headers (Home screen, Click behavior, HTTP cache) with lsep dividers
LANGUAGE COMPLETENESS: - Added 58 missing translations to STRINGS dict for previously-untranslated user-visible Arabic strings - All add_item / notify / error calls now translate substring matches automatically - Playback wait dialog status text and cancel hint now respect ui_language (was hardcoded bilingual)
PLAYBACK WAIT DIALOG (lighter & faster): - Background reduced from 6 layered images to 3 — same look, fewer GPU ops on open - Removed duplicate progress bar glow layer (kept the sharp solid fill) - Status text "CONNECTING" and cancel hint use Window.Property — single language at a time - Fanart fade-in animation removed (was 600ms delay, no real visual benefit)
v3.9.0 — Major upgrade: speed + discovery + reliability: NEW FEATURES: - Discover menu: search history, people search (find any actor/director), episode calendar - Search history: last 30 queries cached locally — pick from list instead of retyping - People search: search TMDb for any actor/director and browse their full filmography - Episode calendar: 2 modes — your Trakt watchlist OR all popular shows for the next 14 days - AI subtitle quality feedback: thumbs-down sends data back to DexWorld to improve translations - Plex/Emby/Jellyfin health monitor: background check every 5min, status visible to skin widgets
PERFORMANCE: - SQLite metadata cache (POV-style): catalog re-renders go from ~800ms to ~50ms after first visit - Smart TTL: old movies cached 6 months, ongoing shows refresh on next-air-date - In-memory layer on top of disk for sub-millisecond reads - Fanart.tv integration: lists now show clearart, banner, discart, multi-language clearlogo
INFRASTRUCTURE: - Centralized logging module with automatic API key/token redaction in logs - Per-host rate limiter (token bucket) for TMDb / Trakt / Cinemeta / Fanart.tv — prevents 429s on busy widgets - Auto-tightens rate when upstream returns 429 - TMDb Helper handoff state machine refactored into single class (was 4 scattered properties) - Subtitle files in special://temp now purged after 24h - Meta cache + Fanart.tv expired rows purged hourly by service
NEW SETTINGS: - Fanart.tv enrichment toggle + personal API key field - TMDb API key (required for people search and discover features) - DexWorld API key (for AI subtitles + feedback) - Manual "Clear metadata cache" action button
- Playback wait window poster no longer crops the bottom of taller-than-2:3 posters (Korean / Asian drama covers) - Reverted the ISA auto-injection from earlier 3.8.13 build — streams now play exactly as the provider sends them, no transparent format changes
v3.8.12 — performance & robustness: - Catalogs no longer render half-empty when an upstream returns a transient 502 (single retry on transient HTTP errors) - Removed redundant filesystem syscall on every cache read/write (~60+ saved per catalog page) - TMDb Helper local-DB lookups now run in read-only mode (~1-3ms faster each, ~30-60ms saved per render) - Metadata-source picker preference cached per invocation (~15-30ms saved on 60-item catalogs) - No changes to playback, source picker, or stop/back behaviour from 3.8.11 (plugin.py byte-identical)
v3.8.11: - Fixed: addon no longer re-enters source search after Stop/Back when playback was already in progress (transient onPlayBackError events from Kodi are now ignored unless playback truly failed within the first minute)
v3.8.10: - TMDb Helper integration overhaul: priority bumped, Arabic translation keys, fallback chain - Player now passes poster/fanart/ar_title/ar_showname directly (fewer HTTP calls per click) - Service-monitor fallback: works from widgets where IDs aren't in the URL - Language flips apply without restart - Stronger Stremio manifest validation with clear error messages - Stale User-Agent strings unified to dynamic version - Removed unused duplicate fanart images (-780 KB)
3.7.98 - Fix AIOStreams/Torrentio source discovery: query all stream providers and preserve imdb/tmdb ids for torrent addons. - Source picker now waits for all stream providers instead of stopping after the first addon returns results.
3.7.92 - إصلاح الاستئناف المباشر من Continue Watching: صار يمرّر resume_percent و source_provider_id، ويشغل مسار Player.play عند وجود استئناف حتى يثبت seek مثل النسخ القديمة. - دعم resume_percent في التشغيل التلقائي للأفلام والحلقات، وليس فقط نافذة اختيار المصدر. - تحسين fanart الحقيقي للفيلم/المسلسل في Continue Watching وNext Up وFavorites، مع حفظ الخلفية في الكاش حتى تظهر في الزيارات التالية. 3.7.91 - إصلاح متابعة المشاهدة: عناصر Trakt كانت تُشغّل من بداية الفيديو بدل من الموضع المحفوظ. السبب جذرياً كان في `import_progress`: استدعاء `/sync/playback/movies` و `/sync/playback/episodes` بدون `?extended=full`، فـ Trakt ما يرجع `runtime`، ومنه `position = duration × progress / 100 = 0`. أضفت `?extended=full` لكلا الاستدعاءين. - حماية إضافية لصفوف قديمة في DB ما عندها position دقيق: تمرير `resume_percent` عبر URL/cache ctx، و`_post_start_chores` يحسب الموضع تلقائياً من `player.getTotalTime() × percent / 100` لمّا يبدأ التشغيل. يضمن أن أي صف فيه `percent > 1%` يتم استئنافه صح حتى لو `position` صفر. - استخدام expli­cit none-check بدل `or` للموضع، لتجنب اعتبار `0.0` فعلية كـ "غير محدد". 3.7.90 - إصلاح حرج: NameError "poster_source_mode" كان يفجّر فتح أي عنصر من Continue Watching أو streams (موجود من قبل، اللوج كشفه). الدالة كانت مستخدمة في `_meta_cache_discriminator` بدون استيراد. أُضيف لقائمة الـ imports من art. - إصلاح Next Up: البوسترات والبانرز ما تظهر — كان السبب أن `_trakt_art_bundle` و `_art_bundle_for_ids` يرجعون poster='' في وضع "محلي فقط" (وهو الافتراضي)، رغم أن صفوف Next Up افتراضية وما عندها provider محلي. الحل: تمرير `force_remote=True` لكل استدعاء من Next Up. - اكتشاف روابط Plex/Emby tokenized المنتهية في الكاش وتجاهلها — قبل كان البوستر القديم يعرض حتى لو ما يشتغل، يحجب البحث عن بديل. - نقل تحميل صور Next Up إلى inline بدل background thread، فالصور تظهر من أول فتح بدل ما تحتاج إعادة فتح للقائمة. - استمرار حفظ الصور المحلولة في playback_store حتى الفتحات اللاحقة تكون فورية. 3.7.89 - إصلاح حرج: تغيير مصدر الميتاداتا (Cinemeta/TMDb Helper/Trakt) لإضافات Plex/Plexio/Emby ما كان يأثر فعلياً، والبوسترات تطلع مكسورة. السبب: `_normalize_meta_art_urls` كان يلفّ بوستر TMDb داخل `/photo/:/transcode?url=...` التابع لخادم Plex، وPlex ما يقدر يحلّ URL خارجي. - الآن: لمّا تختار مصدر صريح، الإضافة تحترم البوستر الجديد كما هو وما تعيد كتابته عبر الخادم الأصلي. - إضافة فحص host مطابق لـ `_plexmod_transcode_url` كحماية ثانية: روابط من نطاقات خارجية ما تنلف أبداً عبر Plex transcode. - مسح فوري لطبقة الكاش `_META_MEM` بعد كل تغيير لمصدر الميتاداتا، حتى تظهر النتيجة بدون إعادة فتح Kodi. - إزالة تعريفات مكررة لـ `_meta_mem_key/get/put` من plugin.py. 3.7.83 - Added official DexWorld IPTV and DexWorld Subtitles public service links to Quick Start and support info. - Kept Plexio/StreamBridge as configured/private manifest services without embedding keys.
3.7.82 - DexWorld recommended sources now use public setup sites only (Plexio + StreamBridge/Emby); no private tokens are bundled - New DexHub fan art background - Added bilingual DexWorld support/trial info (@a6ahd) 3.7.73 - Wait screen v6: cinematic full-bleed fanart 45%, 3-layer amber glow poster, glassmorphism card - Wait screen v6: dual-layer progress bar (solid + glow), 12px wave bars, cyan pulse dot - Wait screen v6: cancel hint inline with wave bars, cleaner layout 3.7.72 - Sources: 8 badge slots (was 4), badges 26px tall (was 20px) - Sources: native badge fallback — HDR/DV/Atmos/4K always show without preset JSON - Sources: video_bits + audio_bits exposed as list properties + shown in row 3 - Sources: size chip top-right, quality band redesigned with 100px height - wait screen: setImage() method for poster (fixes blank poster on CDN Plex) - Icons: full premium set — continue, nextup, favorites, sources, add, settings, accounts 3.7.71 - New premium icon set: continue, nextup, favorites, sources, add, settings, accounts, catalogs, search - Provider icons redesigned: Plex (gold), Emby (green), Trakt (red), TMDb (cyan-green) - Search screen fanart: custom DexHub hex-grid background - All icons match DexHub dark aesthetic (charcoal + amber + cyan + purple) 3.7.70 - Wait screen: poster/clearlogo/fanart now applied via setImage() — fixes blank poster with Plex/CDN URLs - Wait screen: clearlogo shown via setImage(), falls back to text title when absent - Resume: _post_start_chores always runs (not conditional); seek retries up to 6x with adaptive wait - Resume: 25s timeout (was 20s) for slow CDN streams to buffer before giving up on seek - HOTFIX: remove broken image:// wrapper that hid all provider icons in 3.7.69 - provider_art: keep relative URL → base_url resolution (harmless, for edge cases) 3.7.69 - New fanart/background: DexHub brand artwork - Nextup: renders immediately, art backfill runs in background thread (no more waiting) - Provider icons: relative logo URLs resolved against base_url (fixes CDN Plex servers) - Provider icons: image:// wrapper for remote logos (Kodi image cache, no re-download) - Providers list: background manifest refresh for entries with missing logo - _clean_art_value: relative paths starting with / now accepted 3.7.68 - TMDb Helper source: hits local DB first (zero network), falls back to live API - Nextup: parallel art resolve + write-back to playback_store (instant on 2nd visit) - Favorites: shared _resolve_row_art chain: row → TMDb Helper DB → trakt → tmdb_direct - Clearlogo no longer overwritten when changing metadata source - _fetch_from_tmdb_helper now tries installed TMDb Helper DB before any API call 3.7.67 - Meta source picker now actually applies on every item (was skipped when item had a poster) - Meta cache purged on source change so the next listing reflects the new pick instantly - Continue Watching: stale-flag on Window(10000) so home rebuilds even after navigating away - Favorites posters: Trakt-only canonical IDs (trakt:N, slug:...) now resolved correctly - Wait screen: properties pushed to global Window(10000) so $INFO actually finds the poster - Wait screen: properties cleared on close to prevent leak between sessions 3.7.66 -
